title: Quiet where you don’t owe anyone an explanation
hero.subtitle: Small women’s gatherings away from noise — no hour-by-hour program, no pressure to perform joy. Soft closeness, calm rhythm, and the sense that you’re not alone.
hero.imageAlt: Ocean, waves, and a distant horizon

founder.label: Who leads
founder.name: Olga
founder.title: I don’t collect routes — I collect states of mind
founder.body: A few years ago I noticed something simple: the brightest trips don’t happen where the plan is perfect — they happen when the people next to you are the kind you want to be silent with at sunset and laugh with until you cry at dinner. That’s how the club started. We walk along the ocean in Santa Barbara on weekends, drive out to Big Sur and Joshua Tree, and once or twice a year we fly far.
founder.signoff: If you’re reading this — you might already be one of us.

notThis.label: What this isn’t
notThis.title: Not a tour. Not team building. Not a contest for attention.
notThis.body: No checklist tourism, no obligation to “open up” on night one. We don’t sell emotions in bulk — we gather rarely and gently so everyone can breathe, sit in silence together, and speak when it feels right.
notThis.closing: If “nothing to prove” sounds like your speed — this is us.

flow.label: The arc of the day
flow.title: Not a schedule — a mood
flow.intro: The pace follows light and feeling. Below isn’t timing — it’s what first-timers often describe.

steps.title: Morning without a harsh entry
steps.text: Coffee, a window, a short walk. No one waits for you to step on stage.

steps.title: Conversations that grow on their own
steps.text: Light at first — depth when it comes. No forced “let’s go around the circle.”

steps.title: Pauses without guilt
steps.text: Step away, rest, skip the group beat — that’s respected. Boundaries are baseline.

steps.title: An evening you don’t grade
steps.text: Quiet, laughter, or both. The day ends softly — no debrief on how well you performed.

items.caption: Road without a mission to tick every box. Window down, music low, thoughts settle on their own.
items.alt: View from a car toward the horizon

items.caption: Coffee for no reason — just stopping when stopping asks to happen.
items.alt: Hands holding a coffee cup at a table

items.caption: Water nearby: steps, wind, sparse words. You can walk side by side in silence.
Light that simplifies everything. Sunset not for content — to exhale.
Evening quiet when the inner city finally lowers its volume.

community.label: People
community.title: You won’t feel alone — and you don’t have to be “on”
community.body: Often it’s those tired of noise who still want the world nearby. High empathy, care for someone else’s pause, and not grading another’s silence — that’s what holds us together.
community.note: If you fear awkwardness: by evening it usually loosens — not because “everyone’s best friends,” but because performing falls away.

items.title: Small group
items.text: Enough to feel company, small enough not to disappear.

items.title: No performance
items.text: You can be the quiet one, the two-at-the-window talker — both are welcome.

items.title: A spine, not a cage
items.text: Anchors for where to go and what to eat — without sprinting or falling behind.

trips.label: Lines & destinations
trips.title: Choose the tone — we’ll describe the rhythm

items.title: By the water: ocean weekends
items.description: Walks, wind, slow-tempo talk. When you need to shed urban dryness.

items.title: California-close: mini getaways
items.description: Road, changing scenery, evenings with no mandatory plan. A few days in another life.

items.title: Far & rare: bigger trips
items.description: For wide horizons — with the same soft agreement inside the group.

items.title: Bachelorette: quiet celebration
items.description: No dress contest, no “louder” script. Beauty, ease, closeness.

items.title: With children
items.description: When you need air but your child stays right there. Room to be both mother and yourself.

gallery.label: From trips
gallery.title: Frames that stayed
gallery.body: Not a catalog, not a report. Just moments where staying a bit longer felt right.

booking.label: Closest entry
booking.title: An ocean walk in Santa Barbara
booking.body: A few hours by the water, without tour pace or the feeling that you need to tick boxes. Choose a time in the calendar — Olga will confirm the details personally.
booking.note: Times in the calendar reflect real weekend walk slots.

testimonials.label: Reflections
testimonials.title: In a few words — what loosens inside

items.quote: I feared I’d have to perform. For the first time in a year I didn’t feel watched.
items.name: Marina

items.quote: It wasn’t “flawless logistics” — it was calm. Like time turned down a few notches.
items.name: Alina

items.quote: I was quiet half the day and didn’t feel I was doing it wrong. That’s rare — and precious.
items.name: Sophia

cta.label: Next — personal
cta.title: If it resonates, say hi. No mailing lists, no pushy follow-ups.
cta.body: Mention what pulls you in — water, road, city, quiet. We’ll reply like humans, and only if the format really fits.

channels.note: Any channel works. Olga replies personally — no funnels, no auto-messages.

footer.tagline: Cashmere Coast — quiet getaways and a club that breathes slower than the city.
